data.title: Netflix Is 'Not a Screaming Buy' despite Selloff, Says Investor
data.description:
Shares of Netflix (NFLX) have fallen sharply since October, after the video streaming platform emerged as a leading bidder for entertainment rival Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD). The stock is down about 28% in less than three months and roughly one-third from its June 30 peak, yet some investors still believe it is not cheap enough to step in. For example, Christopher Brown of Synovus Securities told Bloomberg that Netflix is "not a screaming buy" at current levels, even though he personally owns the stock and it is held in Synovus portfolios.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Indeed, even after the selloff, Netflix is trading at about 28 times forward earnings, which is higher than rivals like Walt Disney (DIS), Amazon (AMZN), Alphabet (GOOGL), and Paramount Skydance (PSKY). Still, Netflix's valuation is below its own five-year average multiple of 34, which makes the stock look cheaper than it has historically. However, uncertainty around the Warner Bros. deal, valued at $82.7 billion, has become a major overhang, as investors worry about cost, regulatory risk, and Netflix's limited experience with large acquisitions.

Those concerns have made Netflix one of the worst performers in the Nasdaq 100 since late June, and some analysts remain cautious. For instance, Wedbush's Joel Kulina told Bloomberg that the deal could weigh on the stock for months. Meanwhile, CFRA recently downgraded Netflix to a Hold rating due to the risks tied to Warner Bros.' high debt and the shift away from Netflix's long-standing strategy of avoiding big acquisitions.

Is NFLX Stock a Good Buy?

Overall, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on NFLX stock based on 26 Buys, nine Holds, and two Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average NFLX price target of $130.17 per share implies 46.3% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Trump Calls for a One-Year 10% Cap on Credit Card Interest Rates, Draws Sharp Reactions
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump said he is calling for a 10% cap on credit card interest rates for one year. In his social media post, Trump said the rule would take effect on January 20, coinciding with the one-year anniversary of his administration. However, his social media post didn't provide any details about how the rule would be implemented. The president's proposal drew sharp reactions from U.S. Senator Elizabeth Warren, banking advocacy groups, and billionaire fund manager Bill Ackman.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Reactions to Trump's 10% Cap on Credit Card Interest Rates

Trump's move follows criticism from opposition lawmakers for failing to fulfill his campaign promise to address high credit card interest rates.

In his Truth Social post on Friday, Trump said that he will no longer let Americans be "ripped off" by credit card companies that are charging interest rates of 20% to 30%, and even higher, "which festered unimpeded during the Sleepy Joe Biden Administration."

Warren, a Democrat on the Senate Banking Committee, said that Trump's call doesn't make any sense until a bill is passed by Congress. "Begging credit card companies to play nice is a joke. I said a year ago if Trump was serious, I'd work to pass a bill to cap rates," said Warren.

Meanwhile, Ackman expressed concern that the 10% cap could lead to the cancellation of cards for millions of Americans, as credit card companies will not be able to "price subprime credit risk" at this rate.

Bank Advocacy Groups Express Concerns

Banking groups, including the Consumer Bankers Association, the Bank Policy Institute, the American Bankers Association, the Financial Services Forum, and the Independent Community Bankers of America, issued a joint statement in response to Trump's call for a 10% interest rate cap.

They contend that such a move would push consumers toward less-regulated, more expensive alternative sources of credit. They added that there is evidence that such a cap would reduce credit availability and adversely affect millions of American families and small business owners who depend on credit cards.

Trump's call for a 10% cap, if implemented, would negatively impact major U.S. banks and credit card issuers. Meanwhile, banks and credit card issuers haven't yet responded to the proposed 10% cap. Let's look at Wall Street's ratings for some of these companies.

Currently, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Citi (C), Capital One Financial (COF), and Bank of America (BAC), and is cautiously optimistic on JPMorgan Chase (JPM), American Express (AXP), and Wells Fargo (WFC).
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Stock Market News Review: SPY Secures Record High on Unemployment Rate Drop and Rising Consumer Sentiment
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) finished the second week of 2026 on a high note, with the former clinching a new record-high close of $694.07.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

This morning, the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) announced that the unemployment rate was 4.4% in December, below the consensus estimate of 4.5%. In addition, the BLS revised November's unemployment rate to 4.5% from 4.6%.

It wasn't all good news, as December's nonfarm payrolls clocked in at 50,000, which was below the estimate of 70,000. On top of that, October and November's payrolls were revised lower by a combined 76,000 jobs. For 2025, the U.S. economy added 584,000 jobs, registering the worst year for job gains outside of a recession since 2003.

"You don't want to become unemployed, because if you do, it's very difficult to get back in," said Moody's Analytics Chief Economist Mark Zandi. "The duration of unemployment is now becoming quite long." Rate cuts can stimulate the labor market, although the odds of a 25 bps rate cut at the January 28 Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting more than halved to 5% from 11.1% a day ago on CME's FedWatch tool.

Elsewhere, the University of Michigan announced that January's preliminary Index of Consumer Sentiment was 54, higher than the estimate of 53.5 and improving from 52.9 in December. At the same time, long-run inflation expectations rose by 0.2% to 3.4% and surpassed the range of 2.8% to 3.2% in 2025. Year-ahead inflation expectations remained unchanged at 4.2%, the lowest level since January 2025.

"Although consumers' worries about tariffs appear to be gradually receding, they remain guarded about the overall strength of business conditions and labor markets," said Surveys of Consumers Director Joanne Hsu.

Inflation is still a key risk for the economy, said Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta President Raphael Bostic in an interview with the WLRN radio station. Bostic noted that the Fed must remain "laser-focused" on lowering prices and previously stated that he would not support rate cuts in 2026 unless inflation eases.

Finally, investors expecting a Supreme Court ruling on the Trump administration's tariffs will have to wait a bit longer. SCOTUS held its opinion day at 10 a.m. Eastern Time, although the court didn't provide a decision on the case. SCOTUS has scheduled its next opinion day for Wednesday, January 14.

The S&P 500 (SPX) closed with a 0.65% gain, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) returned 1.02%.

Stay ahead of macro events with our up-to-the-minute Economic Calendar — filter by impact, country, and more.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Qualcomm Stock (QCOM) Falls as Top Mizuho Analyst Downgrades Rating on 'Handset Headwinds'
data.description:
Qualcomm (QCOM) stock fell 2.3% on Friday after top Mizuho analyst Vijay Rakesh downgraded the semiconductor company to Hold from Buy, citing challenges in the handset business. The analyst lowered his price target for QCOM stock to $175 from $200. Kumar also expressed concerns about the potential loss of business as key customer Apple (AAPL) is now producing its own modems.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Mizuho Analyst Moves to the Sidelines on QCOM Stock

Rakesh lowered his Fiscal 2026 and Fiscal 2027 revenue and EPS (earnings per share) projections, with his revised estimates falling below the Street's consensus. The 5-star analyst now expects Qualcomm to deliver revenue and EPS of $45.9 billion and $12.12, respectively, for Fiscal 2026 and $46.5 billion and $12.73 for Fiscal 2027.

While Rakesh expects Qualcomm to benefit from expansion in non-handset businesses (such as auto and IoT), he is concerned that the stock's upside could be limited by challenges in the handset industry in 2026. He highlighted that the handset business accounted for more than 70% of the revenue of the company's QCT (Qualcomm CDMA Technology) business. Rakesh expects QCOM to be impacted by a 0-2% decline in global handset shipments.

In particular, Rakesh projects a $2 billion to $3 billion revenue headwind for QCOM, as he expects iPhone units to decline by 7% in 2026. The analyst also flagged the potential loss of business as Apple is now producing its own modems. "For QCOM, we believe the lower exposure to market leader Apple remains a key headwind for 2026E and beyond," said Rakesh. He highlighted that the iPhone maker contributed $8.8 billion in revenue for Qualcomm in Fiscal 2025, and the loss of modem business could put about $3 billion at risk.  

Meanwhile, Rakesh named Nvidia (NVDA), Lumentum (LITE), and Broadcom (AVGO) as Mizuho's top picks for 2026, backed by secular trends in artificial intelligence (AI) accelerators, networking, and memory. Additionally, he highlighted two key "outperformers," Microchip Technology (MCHP) and Lam Research (LRCX), given their strategic exposure to wafer fabrication and AI-related growth. While Rakesh is positive about the semiconductor sector in the first half of 2026, he is cautious about the second half of the year due to "1) higher volatility around new product launches/ramps with Rubin/ MI400, 2) US midterm elections (11/3/2026), and 3) slower Fed rate cuts."

Is QCOM Stock a Good Buy?

Overall, Wall Street is cautious on Qualcomm stock based on 10 Buys, five Holds, and one Sell recommendation. The average QCOM stock price target of $197.14 indicates 11% upside potential. QCOM stock has risen 12% in the past year.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Nvidia Stock Forecast (NVDA) – The Path from $4.6T to a $10T Market Cap by 2030
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) stock climbed nearly 35% over the past year, pushing its market cap to nearly $4.6 trillion. Investors now wonder about the company's path by decade's end. Will intensifying competition or sales hits from China export bans stall Nvidia's momentum? The outlook remains promising if Nvidia sustains its market dominance and keeps rolling out cutting-edge AI products, as it did at the recent CES 2026 event.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Nvidia made several key AI-focused announcements at CES 2026 during CEO Jensen Huang's keynote. The most noteworthy was Alpamayo, an open-source AI model for self-driving cars that enables human-like thinking to reason, predict, and navigate in tough conditions. The semiconductor giant also launched upgrades to its Vera CPU and Rubin GPU, boosting single-threaded speed, memory size, and data center power. Importantly, Nvidia confirmed that production of its next-gen Rubin architecture is proceeding at full speed.

Nvidia Has Generated Astronomical Returns

Over the past decade, Nvidia delivered over 23,000% growth as AI shifted from hype to reality. A $1,000 investment on December 31, 2015, would now be worth about $231,000.

The AI market continues expanding with fresh products and applications. Nvidia has also evolved from a gaming-focused GPU maker to the go-to provider of parallel processors powering AI, launching innovations at a steady clip. Hyperscalers' huge AI bets have not yet fully paid off, and tech giants envision AI automating routine tasks across industries over the coming years.

Nvidia Holds a Strong Lead Over Competitors

Nvidia's impressive past growth may not persist indefinitely at the same pace, as mature businesses typically see their expansion rates slow over time. In the quarter ended October 26, 2025, total revenue topped $50 billion for the first time, driven by $51.2 billion in Data Center sales, showing a 66% year-over-year jump.

Nvidia captures a large share of Big Tech's AI budgets. Amazon (AMZN), Alphabet (GOOGL), Microsoft (MSFT), and Meta (META) were expected to spend $600 billion on AI infrastructure in 2025, with outlays rising in 2026 and beyond. Nvidia is expected to claim about $200 billion of those budgets.

How Will Nvidia Get to a $10 Trillion Market Cap?

Nvidia forecasts $3 trillion to $4 trillion in global data center capex by 2030. Even if rivals grab a slice, $600 billion in revenue seems largely achievable. Nvidia's current 50% profit margins translate to $300 billion in net income, and applying a 33x earnings multiple points to a $10 trillion market cap.

Nvidia trades around $189 per share today ($4.60 trillion market cap). Reaching $10 trillion by 2030 requires $410 per share, suggesting a 117% upside potential. A leading Wall Street analyst, Mark Lipacis of Evercore ISI, already projects a $352 price target over the next twelve months. This simply shows us that $410 is not a pipe dream.

Does NVDA Stock Have More Upside from Here?

Analysts are highly optimistic about Nvidia's growth ahead. Their average Nvidia price target of $263.76 implies 39.5% upside potential from current levels over the next twelve months.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Chevron Stock Forecast (CVX): Here's Wall Street's Take on Venezuela Situation and Q4 Expectations
data.description:
Oil prices have been volatile due to the ongoing political developments in Venezuela following the U.S. capture of Nicolas Maduro and the Trump administration's announcement of an initial $2 billion deal to supply Venezuelan oil to the U.S. and possibly other countries. Energy major Chevron (CVX) has been in focus as it is currently the only foreign company authorized by the U.S. to export Venezuelan oil. During the White House meeting between U.S. President Donald Trump and executives of major energy giants, Chevron Vice Chairman Mark Nelson said that the company is ready to boost liftings at its joint ventures with state oil company PDVSA by 100% immediately, Reuters reported.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"We are also able to increase our production within our own disciplined investment schemes by about 50% just in the next 18 to 24 months," said Nelson.

Let's look at Wall Street's views on Chevron stock, with the Venezuela situation in focus and Q4 results approaching. Chevron is slated to announce its Q4 2025 results on January 30. Wall Street expects the company to report earnings per share (EPS) of $1.47, down 29% from the prior-year quarter.

Meanwhile, on Thursday, Chevron stock gained 2.6% on reports that the company is in talks with the U.S. government to expand a key license to operate in Venezuela, so that it can boost crude exports to its own refineries and sell to other buyers.

Bank of America Weighs in on Chevron Stock

Commenting on the Venezuela situation, Bank of America analyst Jean Ann Salisbury noted that the country now produces about 1 mbd, or only 1% of global oil, much of which has been embargoed in recent weeks. She added that if the embargo is lifted, it will drag down oil prices in the near term. That said, further ground-level conflict could disrupt production and push oil prices higher.

Salisbury noted that Chevron is the only U.S. company still operating in Venezuela, with the country accounting for about 2% of the oil and gas giant's cash flow. She views Chevron as the "prime beneficiary of potential new developments given its existing position." Salisbury reiterated a Buy rating on Chevron stock with a price target of $180.

It is worth noting that the analyst cautioned about the higher costs associated with Venezuelan barrels and high emission intensity. Salisbury pointed out that most of Venezuela's oil is very heavy and requires massive infrastructure to produce, with most experts estimating tens of billions of dollars and years to rebuild the country's oil industry.

Views of Other Wall Street Analysts on CVX Stock

Meanwhile, Mizuho analyst Nitin Kumar reiterated a Buy rating on Chevron stock with a price target of $206. The analyst expects Chevron's Q4 2025 earnings call to focus on the possible opportunities in Venezuela. Kumar sees the possibility that Chevron's Q4 EPS (earnings per share) and CFPS (cash flow per share) could miss consensus estimates by 8% to 9%. He expects CVX's Q4 results to reflect the impact of the CPC pipeline outage and weaker commodity prices.

Overall, Kumar thinks that the focus is on Chevron's future, with the company already announcing a budget of $18 billion to $19 billion for 2026, at the low end of the long-term (2030) outlook of $18 billion to $21 billion.

Additionally, BMO Capital analyst Phillip Jungwirth reiterated a Buy rating on Chevron with a price target of $170, saying, "The Venezuela situation is highly uncertain, but Chevron is well positioned here, and its operations continue uninterrupted, lifting ~240MBD." Jungwirth expects Chevron to deliver mixed Q4 2025 results, given headwinds from TCO and El Segundo, as well as lower crude prices. Nonetheless, the analyst remains positive on Chevron's longer-term outlook and enhanced portfolio following the Hess acquisition.

Is CVX Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Overall, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Chevron stock based on 15 Buys and four Hold recommendations. The average CVX stock price target of $176.95 indicates 9.15% upside potential. CVX stock has risen 8% over the past year. Chevron offers a dividend yield of 4.5%.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Why is the Stock Market Up Today, 1/9/2026?
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) are off to a strong start in 2026, with both indexes trading higher on Friday.

The market got a boost after the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) reported that the unemployment rate in December was 4.4%, below the consensus estimate of 4.5%. In addition, the BLS revised November's unemployment rate to 4.5% from 4.6%.

Consumer Sentiment Tops Expectations

Furthermore, the University of Michigan's preliminary Index of Consumer Sentiment (ICS) for January was 54, higher than the consensus estimate of 53.5 and rising from 52.9 in December. At the same time, the reading is still down by 24.7% year-over-year from 71.7. A reading below 60 signals pessimism, while a reading above 70 signals optimism.

Still, the ICS has now risen for two consecutive months and is at its highest level since September 2025. Surveys of Consumers Director Joanne Hsu added that concerns about tariffs are beginning to recede, although consumers remain cautious about the state of the labor market.

data.title: Amazon Stock Climbs Amid Layoffs Chatter — Here's What Lloyd Walmsley Sees Next
data.description:
Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) stock opened 2026 amid renewed discussion around workforce reductions, but the stock has shown little sign of stress. Despite some headlines resurfacing around potential layoffs, shares closed the first full trading week of the year up ~6%.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The muted reaction makes more sense once you filter out the rumors and stick to what Amazon has actually said. The company confirmed in late 2025 that it was eliminating about 14,000 corporate roles, or roughly 4% of its non-warehouse workforce, as part of a broader efficiency push. Reports pointing to far deeper cuts – in some cases as many as 30,000 positions – continue to circulate, but those figures have not been officially backed by Amazon.

One way or another, investors generally view layoffs at scale not as a red flag, but as a lever for margin expansion, particularly for a company already operating at Amazon's size. With consensus EPS estimates for fiscal 2026 hovering around $7.85, the question is whether efficiency gains can meaningfully lift earnings without undermining execution. This is where the bull case shifts toward AWS.

One of the most optimistic voices on the Street belongs to Mizuho analyst Lloyd Walmsley, who sees Amazon's cloud business entering a new phase of AI-driven growth. Walmsley, who's ranked among the top 2% on Wall Street, argues that investor expectations for AWS remain too conservative, particularly as AI workloads evolve. In his view, the industry is moving away from training-heavy demand toward inference, a shift that plays directly to AWS's strengths in custom silicon and cost-efficient infrastructure.

Walmsley believes Amazon's AI-related revenue could double in 2026, reaching about $17 billion, as inference adoption accelerates and capacity constraints ease. The top analyst also points to Amazon's expanding lineup of custom chips, including Trainium and Graviton, as a key advantage, helping customers lower costs while scaling AI workloads. In his words, AWS is increasingly positioned to "support AI at scale" in a way that few competitors can match.

Importantly, Walmsley expects this momentum to extend beyond a single year. As more enterprises migrate legacy workloads to the cloud and layer AI capabilities on top, AWS growth could re-accelerate through the second half of 2026 and beyond.

That combination of cloud migration, AI inference, and operating leverage underpins Walmsley's Outperform (i.e., Buy) rating on AMZN shares, while his $300 price target implies 27% upside. (To watch Walmsley's track record, click here)

Walmsley is hardly the only analyst impressed with Amazon. The stock has 46 Buy recommendations and just 1 Hold, earning it a Strong Buy consensus rating. The $294.55 average price target suggests 19% upside potential in the coming months. (See AMZN stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel
